---
title: "dnsmasq to update resolv.conf"
date: 2013-04-09
forum: Networking &amp; Wireless
---

### Post by mario.almeida on 2013-04-09
dnsmasq do not update /etc/resolv.conf for dns server details provided by DHCP severs

/etc/resolv.conf 
```
# Dynamic resolv.conf(5) file for glibc resolver(3) generated by resolvconf(8)
#     DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE BY HAND -- YOUR CHANGES WILL BE OVERWRITTEN
nameserver 127.0.1.1
```

From network manager -> Connection Information I can see the correct DNS details from DHCP server but changes are not reflected.


When I do host lookup the query is sent to 8.8.8.8 instead my local dns server.

---

### Post by jdthood on 2013-04-10
> **mario.almeida said:**
> dnsmasq do not update /etc/resolv.conf for dns server details provided by DHCP severs

Dnsmasq does not and is not supposed to update the resolv.conf file.

When the NetworkManager-controlled dnsmasq instance is running, resolv.conf contains "nameserver 127.0.0.1" so that the resolver will direct DNS queries to the NetworkManager-controlled dnsmasq instance. NetworkManager sends the upstream nameserver addresses directly to its slave dnsmasq instance over D-Bus.

Links of possible interest to you:
  [http://askubuntu.com/questions/180094/is-there-a-way-to-make-dig-report-the-actual-name-server-rather-than-127-0-0-1](http://askubuntu.com/questions/180094/is-there-a-way-to-make-dig-report-the-actual-name-server-rather-than-127-0-0-1)
  [http://askubuntu.com/questions/129089/dns-server-address-no-longer-stored-in-resolv-conf-where-is-it-now](http://askubuntu.com/questions/129089/dns-server-address-no-longer-stored-in-resolv-conf-where-is-it-now)
